### Custom Installation Directory Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/README.md

Examples of setting custom installation directories for OpenCode.

```bash
OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=/usr/local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

```bash
XDG_BIN_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

--------------------------------

### Request example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of an API request using cURL.

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://api.example.com/users' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"name": "John Doe", "email": "john@example.com"}'
```

--------------------------------

### Response example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of a JSON API response.

```json
{
  "id": "user_123",
  "name": "John Doe", 
  "email": "john@example.com",
  "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
}
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with custom initialization

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example demonstrating how to use a custom `init` function to perform setup and return extra data accessible via `tmp.extra`.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir<string>({
  init: async (dir) => {
    await Bun.write(path.join(dir, "file.txt"), "content")
    return "extra data"
  },
})
// Access extra data via tmp.extra
console.log(tmp.extra) // "extra data"
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (Linux)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on Linux using apt.

```bash
sudo apt install nodejs npm
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/README.md

Command to install project dependencies using Bun.

```bash
bun install
```

--------------------------------

### Advanced configuration example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

An example of a JavaScript configuration object within an accordion.

```javascript
const config = {
  performance: { cache: true, timeout: 30000 },
  security: { encryption: 'AES-256' }
};
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (Windows)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on Windows using Chocolatey.

```powershell
choco install nodejs
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Mise

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Mise.

```bash
mise use -g github:anomalyco/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode using Homebrew

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on macOS and Linux using Homebrew.

```bash
brew install anomalyco/tap/opencode
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Scoop

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Scoop.

```bash
scoop install opencode
```

### Install OpenCode on Windows using NPM

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using NPM.

```bash
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

### Configure environment step

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of configuring environment variables within a step.

```bash
API_KEY=your_api_key_here
```

--------------------------------

### Single code block example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of a single code block in JavaScript.

```javascript
const apiConfig = {
  baseURL: "https://api.example.com",
  timeout: 5000,
  headers: {
    Authorization: `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`,
  },
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install dependencies (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Commands to install Node.js and a package on macOS using Homebrew.

```bash
brew install node
npm install -g package-name
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Arch Linux

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Arch Linux using pacman or paru.

```bash
sudo pacman -S opencode
```

```bash
paru -S opencode-bin
```

--------------------------------

### Install OpenCode on Windows using Chocolatey

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Install OpenCode on Windows using Chocolatey.

```bash
choco install opencode
```

### Install SDK

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Install the opencode SDK from npm.

```bash
npm install @opencode-ai/sdk
```

--------------------------------

### Example Request-context Call

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/specs/v2/api.html

An example of a GET request to retrieve a file system tree, specifying a path, directory, and workspace.

```HTTP
GET /api/fs/tree?path=.&directory=/repo/app&workspace=ws_123
```

--------------------------------

### Required YAML frontmatter

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of the YAML frontmatter required at the beginning of every documentation page.

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific, keyword-rich title"
description: "Concise description explaining page purpose and value"
---
```

--------------------------------

### Install Mintlify CLI

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/README.md

Command to install the Mintlify CLI globally using npm.

```bash
npm i -g mint
```

--------------------------------

### Example .windsurf/rules.md file

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/windsurf.mdx

A markdown file defining workspace rules for Windsurf's AI assistant, providing context about the Mintlify documentation project and writing standards. This file should be created in your project root.

```markdown
# Mintlify technical writing rule

## Project context

- This is a documentation project on the Mintlify platform
- We use MDX files with YAML frontmatter
- Navigation is configured in `docs.json`
- We follow technical writing best practices

## Writing standards

- Use second person ("you") for instructions
- Write in active voice and present tense
- Start procedures with prerequisites
- Include expected outcomes for major steps
- Use descriptive, keyword-rich headings
- Keep sentences concise but informative

## Required page structure

Every page must start with frontmatter:

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific title"
description: "Concise description for SEO and navigation"
---
```

## Mintlify components

### Callouts

- `<Note>` for helpful supplementary information
- `<Warning>` for important cautions and breaking changes
- `<Tip>` for best practices and expert advice
- `<Info>` for neutral contextual information
- `<Check>` for success confirmations

### Code examples

- When appropriate, include complete, runnable examples
- Use `<CodeGroup>` for multiple language examples
- Specify language tags on all code blocks
- Include realistic data, not placeholders
- Use `<RequestExample>` and `<ResponseExample>` for API docs

### Procedures

- Use `<Steps>` component for sequential instructions
- Include verification steps with `<Check>` components when relevant
- Break complex procedures into smaller steps

### Content organization

- Use `<Tabs>` for platform-specific content
- Use `<Accordion>` for progressive disclosure
- Use `<Card>` and `<CardGroup>` for highlighting content
- Wrap images in `<Frame>` components with descriptive alt text

## API documentation requirements

- Document all parameters with `<ParamField>`
- Show response structure with `<ResponseField>`
- Include both success and error examples
- Use `<Expandable>` for nested object properties
- Always include authentication examples

## Quality standards

- Test all code examples before publishing
- Use relative paths for internal links
- Include alt text for all images
- Ensure proper heading hierarchy (start with h2)
- Check existing patterns for consistency

```

--------------------------------

### OpenAPI Configuration Examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/essentials/settings.mdx

Examples demonstrating different ways to configure the 'openapi' property.

```json
"openapi": "https://example.com/openapi.json"
```

```json
"openapi": "/openapi.json"
```

```json
"openapi": ["https://example.com/openapi1.json", "/openapi2.json", "/openapi3.json"]
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode web interface

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Starts a headless OpenCode server with a web interface.

```bash
opencode web
```

--------------------------------

### Install a plugin

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Install a plugin and update your config.

```bash
opencode plugin <module>
```

--------------------------------

### Start OpenCode server

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Starts a headless OpenCode server for API access.

```bash
opencode serve
```

--------------------------------

### Import session data examples

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Examples of importing session data from a local file and a share URL.

```bash
opencode import session.json
opencode import https://opncd.ai/s/abc123
```

--------------------------------

### Install a plugin (alias)

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Use the alias to install a plugin.

```bash
opencode plug <module>
```

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Start the OpenCode TUI, optionally specifying a project path.

```bash
opencode [project]
```

curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash

A practical example of using `opencode run` with a specific prompt.

```bash
opencode run Explain the use of context in Go
```

Change directory to your project.

```bash
cd /path/to/project
```

Run OpenCode using Docker.

```bash
docker run -it --rm ghcr.io/anomalyco/opencode

Run the /init command to analyze your project and create AGENTS.md.

```bash
/init
```

```bash
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
bun install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
pnpm install -g opencode-ai
```

```bash
yarn global add opencode-ai
```

npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code

### Ask OpenCode a question

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of asking OpenCode to explain a part of the codebase.

```txt
@packages/functions/src/api/index.ts
How is authentication handled in @packages/functions/src/api/index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Config API Get and Providers

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Examples of getting configuration information using `config.get()` and listing providers and default models using `config.providers()`.

```javascript
const config = await client.config.get()

const { providers, default: defaults } = await client.config.providers()
```

--------------------------------

### Required page frontmatter example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/windsurf.mdx

Example of the YAML frontmatter required at the beginning of every documentation page, as specified in the workspace rules.

```yaml
---
title: "Clear, specific title"
description: "Concise description for SEO and navigation"
---
```

--------------------------------

### Partial Service Stub Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of using Layer.mock to create a partial service stub, overriding only necessary methods.

```typescript
import { Effect, Layer } from "effect"
import { Account } from "@/account/account"

const failingAccountLayer = Layer.mock(Account.Service, {
  orgsByAccount: () => Effect.fail(new Account.AccountServiceError({ message: "simulated upstream failure" })),
})
```

--------------------------------

### Describe the feature in Plan mode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of describing a new feature to OpenCode in Plan mode.

```txt
When a user deletes a note, we'd like to flag it as deleted in the database.
Then create a screen that shows all the recently deleted notes.
From this screen, the user can undelete a note or permanently delete it.
```

--------------------------------

### Paste API key

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of the prompt to paste your API key.

```txt
┌ API key
│
│
└ enter
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with Git repository

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of initializing a temporary directory as a Git repository.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir({ git: true })
```

--------------------------------

### Reviewing a Pull Request

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/src/command/template/review.txt

Commands to get context and diff for a pull request.

```bash
gh pr view $ARGUMENTS
```

```bash
gh pr diff $ARGUMENTS
```

--------------------------------

### Path API Get Current Path

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Example of using `path.get()` to retrieve current path information.

```javascript
// Get current path information
const pathInfo = await client.path.get()
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with config file

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of creating a temporary directory with a pre-configured `opencode.json` file.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir({
  config: { model: "test/model", username: "testuser" },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Create and manage sessions

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Examples demonstrating how to create, list, prompt, and inject context into sessions using the SDK.

```javascript
// Create and manage sessions
const session = await client.session.create({
  body: { title: "My session" },
})

const sessions = await client.session.list()

// Send a prompt message
const result = await client.session.prompt({
  path: { id: session.id },
  body: {
    model: { providerID: "anthropic", modelID: "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" },
    parts: [{ type: "text", text: "Hello!" }],
  },
})

// Inject context without triggering AI response (useful for plugins)
await client.session.prompt({
  path: { id: session.id },
  body: {
    noReply: true,
    parts: [{ type: "text", text: "You are a helpful assistant." }],
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Example of attaching TUI to a running backend

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/cli.mdx

Demonstrates starting a web backend and then attaching the TUI from another terminal.

```bash
# Start the backend server for web/mobile access
opencode web --port 4096 --hostname 0.0.0.0

# In another terminal, attach the TUI to the running backend
opencode attach http://10.20.30.40:4096
```

--------------------------------

### Run the project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/README.md

Command to run the project's main script using Bun.

```bash
bun run index.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Project API List and Current

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/sdk.mdx

Examples of listing all projects using `project.list()` and getting the current project using `project.current()`.

```javascript
// List all projects
const projects = await client.project.list()

// Get current project
const currentProject = await client.project.current()
```

--------------------------------

### Code group with multiple languages

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/cursor.mdx

Example of a code group demonstrating API calls in Node.js, Python, and cURL.

```javascript
const response = await fetch('/api/endpoint', {
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${apiKey}` }
});
```

```python
import requests
response = requests.get('/api/endpoint',
  headers={'Authorization': f'Bearer {api_key}'})
```

```curl
curl -X GET '/api/endpoint' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY'
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to LLM provider

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Run the /connect command in the TUI to configure an LLM provider.

```txt
/connect
```

--------------------------------

### tmpdir with custom cleanup

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example showing how to use a custom `dispose` function for specific cleanup logic.

```typescript
await using tmp = await tmpdir({
  init: async (dir) => {
    const specialDir = path.join(dir, "special")
    await fs.mkdir(specialDir)
    return specialDir
  },
  dispose: async (dir) => {
    // Custom cleanup logic
    await fs.rm(path.join(dir, "special"), { recursive: true })
  },
})
```

--------------------------------

### CLAUDE.md file content

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/docs/ai-tools/claude-code.mdx

Example content for the CLAUDE.md file, used to train Claude Code on documentation standards.

```markdown
# Mintlify documentation

## Working relationship

- You can push back on ideas-this can lead to better documentation. Cite sources and explain your reasoning when you do so
- ALWAYS ask for clarification rather than making assumptions
- NEVER lie, guess, or make up information

## Project context

- Format: MDX files with YAML frontmatter
- Config: docs.json for navigation, theme, settings
- Components: Mintlify components

## Content strategy

- Document just enough for user success - not too much, not too little
- Prioritize accuracy and usability of information
- Make content evergreen when possible
- Search for existing information before adding new content. Avoid duplication unless it is done for a strategic reason
- Check existing patterns for consistency
- Start by making the smallest reasonable changes

## Frontmatter requirements for pages

- title: Clear, descriptive page title
- description: Concise summary for SEO/navigation

## Writing standards

- Second-person voice ("you")
- Prerequisites at start of procedural content
- Test all code examples before publishing
- Match style and formatting of existing pages
- Include both basic and advanced use cases
- Language tags on all code blocks
- Alt text on all images
- Relative paths for internal links

## Git workflow

- NEVER use --no-verify when committing
- Ask how to handle uncommitted changes before starting
- Create a new branch when no clear branch exists for changes
- Commit frequently throughout development
- NEVER skip or disable pre-commit hooks

## Do not

- Skip frontmatter on any MDX file
- Use absolute URLs for internal links
- Include untested code examples
- Make assumptions - always ask for clarification
```

--------------------------------

### Switch to Plan mode

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Command to switch OpenCode to Plan mode using the Tab key.

```bash
<TAB>
```

--------------------------------

### Using TestInstance with it.instance

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Example of yielding `TestInstance` to access the temporary directory path within an `it.instance` test.

```typescript
import { TestInstance } from "../fixture/fixture"

it.instance("uses the temp directory", () =>
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const test = yield* TestInstance
    expect(test.directory).toContain("opencode-test-")
  }),
)
```

--------------------------------

### Agent Browser Snapshot Interactive Elements

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/app/AGENTS.md

Get interactive elements with references (@e1, @e2) using `agent-browser`.

```shell
agent-browser snapshot -i
```

--------------------------------

### Flow configuration

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/gitlab.mdx

Example of a flow configuration file for OpenCode in GitLab CI/CD, including installation, authentication, Git configuration, and OpenCode execution logic.

```yaml
image: node:22-slim
commands:
  - echo "Installing opencode"
  - npm install --global opencode-ai
  - echo "Installing glab"
  - export GITLAB_TOKEN=$GITLAB_TOKEN_OPENCODE
  - apt-get update --quiet && apt-get install --yes curl wget gpg git && rm --recursive --force /var/lib/apt/lists/*
  - curl --silent --show-error --location "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/upciti/wakemeops/main/assets/install_repository" | bash
  - apt-get install --yes glab
  - echo "Configuring glab"
  - echo $GITLAB_HOST
  - echo "Creating OpenCode auth configuration"
  - mkdir --parents ~/.local/share/opencode
  - |
    cat > ~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json << EOF
    {
      "anthropic": {
        "type": "api",
        "key": "$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"
      }
    }
    EOF
  - echo "Configuring git"
  - git config --global user.email "opencode@gitlab.com"
  - git config --global user.name "OpenCode"
  - echo "Testing glab"
  - glab issue list
  - echo "Running OpenCode"
  - |
    opencode run "
    You are an AI assistant helping with GitLab operations.

    Context: $AI_FLOW_CONTEXT
    Task: $AI_FLOW_INPUT
    Event: $AI_FLOW_EVENT

    Please execute the requested task using the available GitLab tools.
    Be thorough in your analysis and provide clear explanations.

    <important>
    Please use the glab CLI to access data from GitLab. The glab CLI has already been authenticated. You can run the corresponding commands.

    If you are asked to summarize an MR or issue or asked to provide more information then please post back a note to the MR/Issue so that the user can see it.
    You don't need to commit or push up changes, those will be done automatically based on the file changes you make.
    </important>
    "
  - git checkout --branch $CI_WORKLOAD_REF origin/$CI_WORKLOAD_REF
  - echo "Checking for git changes and pushing if any exist"
  - |
    if ! git diff --quiet || ! git diff --cached --quiet || [ --not --zero "$(git ls-files --others --exclude-standard)" ]; then
      echo "Git changes detected, adding and pushing..."
      git add .
      if git diff --cached --quiet; then
        echo "No staged changes to commit"
      else
        echo "Committing changes to branch: $CI_WORKLOAD_REF"
        git commit --message "Codex changes"
        echo "Pushing changes up to https://gitlab-ci-token:$GITLAB_TOKEN@$GITLAB_HOST/gl-demo-ultimate-dev-ai-epic-17570/test-java-project.git $CI_WORKLOAD_REF"
        git push https://gitlab-ci-token:$GITLAB_TOKEN@$GITLAB_HOST/gl-demo-ultimate-dev-ai-epic-17570/test-java-project.git $CI_WORKLOAD_REF
        echo "Changes successfully pushed"
      fi
    else
      echo "No git changes detected, skipping push"
    fi
variables:
  - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
  - GITLAB_TOKEN_OPENCODE
  - GITLAB_HOST
```

--------------------------------

### Provide feedback or add details to the plan

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of providing feedback or additional details to OpenCode's plan, including referencing an image.

```txt
We'd like to design this new screen using a design I've used before.
[Image #1] Take a look at this image and use it as a reference.
```

--------------------------------

### Create Starlight Project

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/README.md

Command to initialize a new Astro project using the Starlight template.

```bash
npm create astro@latest -- --template starlight
```

--------------------------------

### Synchronization Example: Antipattern vs. Fix

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/opencode/test/AGENTS.md

Comparison of an antipattern using fixed sleep for synchronization and the recommended fix using a published readiness signal.

```ts
// Antipattern — race
yield * prompt.shell({ command: "sleep 30" }).pipe(Effect.forkChild)
yield * Effect.sleep(50)
yield * prompt.cancel(chat.id)

// Fix — wait for a published readiness signal
yield * prompt.shell({ command: "sleep 30" }).pipe(Effect.forkChild)
yield *
  pollWithTimeout(
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      const s = yield* (yield* SessionStatus.Service).get(chat.id)
      return s.type === "busy" ? (true as const) : undefined
    }),
    "session never became busy",
  )
yield * prompt.cancel(chat.id)
```

--------------------------------

### Directly ask OpenCode to make changes

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/web/src/content/docs/index.mdx

Example of asking OpenCode to make straightforward changes without a prior plan review, referencing specific files.

```txt
We need to add authentication to the /settings route. Take a look at how this is
handled in the /notes route in @packages/functions/src/notes.ts and implement
the same logic in @packages/functions/src/settings.ts
```

--------------------------------

### Record and Replay HTTP Requests with Effect

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/http-recorder/README.md

This example demonstrates how to record an HTTP GET request using `HttpRecorder.http` within an Effect test. The first run records the response, and subsequent runs replay it from a JSON cassette.

```ts
import { assert, describe, it } from "@effect/vitest"
import { Effect, Schema } from "effect"
import { HttpClient, HttpClientRequest } from "effect/unstable/http"
import { HttpRecorder } from "@opencode-ai/http-recorder"

const User = Schema.Struct({
  id: Schema.Number,
  name: Schema.String,
})

const getUser = Effect.gen(function* () {
  const http = yield* HttpClient.HttpClient
  const response = yield* http.execute(HttpClientRequest.get("https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/1"))
  return yield* Schema.decodeUnknownEffect(User)(yield* response.json)
})

describe("getUser", () => {
  it.effect("loads a user", () =>
    Effect.gen(function* () {
      const user = yield* getUser

      assert.strictEqual(user.id, 1)
      assert.strictEqual(user.name, "Leanne Graham")
    }).pipe(Effect.provide(HttpRecorder.http("users/get-one"))),
  )
})
```

--------------------------------

### Full `opencode.json` Configuration Example

Source: https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode/blob/dev/packages/core/src/plugin/skill/customize-opencode.md

This snippet shows a comprehensive example of the `opencode.json` configuration file, illustrating various settings for models, skills, agents, commands, providers, plugins, and permissions.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://opencode.ai/config.json",
  "username": "string",
  "model": "provider/model-id",
  "small_model": "provider/model-id",
  "default_agent": "agent-name",
  "shell": "/bin/zsh",
  "logLevel": "DEBUG" | "INFO" | "WARN" | "ERROR",
  "share": "manual" | "auto" | "disabled",
  "autoupdate": true | false | "notify",
  "snapshot": true,
  "instructions": ["AGENTS.md", "docs/style.md"],

  "skills": {
    "paths": [".opencode/skills", "/abs/path/to/skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"]
  },

  "agent": {
    "my-agent": {
      "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "mode": "subagent",
      "description": "...",
      "permission": { "edit": "deny" }
    }
  },

  "command": {
    "deploy": { "description": "...", "prompt": "..." }
  },

  "provider": {
    "anthropic": { "options": { "apiKey": "..." } }
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],

  "mcp": {
    "playwright": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["npx", "-y", "@playwright/mcp"],
      "enabled": true,
      "env": {}
    },
    "remote-thing": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://...",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." }
    }
  },

  "plugin": [
    "opencode-gemini-auth",
    "opencode-foo@1.2.3",
    "./local-plugin.ts",
    ["opencode-bar", { "option": "value" }]
  ],

  "permission": {
    "edit": "deny",
    "bash": { "git *": "allow", "*": "ask" }
  },

  "formatter": false,
  "lsp": false,

  "experimental": {
    "primary_tools": ["edit"],
    "mcp_timeout": 30000
  },

  "tool_output": { "max_lines": 200, "max_bytes": 8192 },

  "compaction": { "auto": true, "tail_turns": 15 }
}
```
